procedure.requested_by: Dr. Sarah Martinez, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Member meets conservative treatment requirements. However, surgical request is premature. Member's symptoms have been present for only 6 weeks. Plan criteria require at least 6 weeks of symptoms before surgical consideration. Recent onset radiculopathy has a high rate of spontaneous resolution.

appeal_basis: Member is a professional musician — right hand weakness is career-ending
All conservative treatment completed and failed within the 6-week period
Physician recommends surgery given functional impairment

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Acute cervical radiculopathy C5-C6 with functional impairment
clinical_record.occupation: Professional violinist — right hand function essential to livelihood

mri_1.findings: C5-C6 large disc herniation with moderate right C6 nerve root compression. Foraminal stenosis. No cord contact. No myelomalacia.

neurological_exam.findings: Right C6 dermatomal numbness, bicep reflex diminished right, grip strength 4/5 right. Fine motor testing: unable to perform violin fingering tasks. Functional impairment documented by occupational therapy.

physical_therapy_summary.note: 6 weeks intensive PT. Functional plateau documented at 6 weeks. No improvement in fine motor deficits or radicular symptoms. OT confirms inability to perform professional duties.

agents.response: Partial pain relief, no motor improvement

right_c6_epidural.response: 25% temporary relief, no improvement in motor deficit, returned to baseline in 2 weeks

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — only 6 weeks of symptoms, plan requires minimum 6 weeks before surgical consideration. Technically borderline at best.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: The plan's 6-week requirement is met exactly — does at least 6 weeks mean >6 weeks or ≥6 weeks?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Clinical standards allow earlier surgery for significant functional impairment — does occupational impairment in a professional musician constitute significant functional impairment under CHSC 1374.32?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: All conservative treatment criteria are met. If the symptom duration criterion is met (exactly 6 weeks), is there any remaining basis for denial?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Does the plan's 6-week minimum apply to symptom duration or to conservative treatment duration?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: The plan denied on symptom duration — only 6 weeks. But 6 weeks is the minimum, and member has exactly met it. All conservative criteria are met. Clinical standard supports earlier surgery for functional impairment in precision-dependent occupations. The plan's premature argument is the weakest possible basis for denial when all criteria are otherwise satisfied.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — All conservative treatment criteria are met (PT to plateau, two agents, injection). The 6-week symptom duration minimum is satisfied. Clinical standards support surgery when functional plateau is reached and significant functional impairment exists — documented by OT evaluation. The recent onset argument does not survive scrutiny when all plan criteria are met and clinical standard supports the procedure.
